title: pultenaea quaerita
intro: Pultenaea quaerita, also known as the rare bush-pea, is a flowering plant in the legume family native to Western Australia. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pultenaea quaerita are said to include soothing sore muscles and skin irritations, as well as providing relief from fever and other ailments. Pultenaea quaerita can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be approached with caution due to limited research and potential side effects.
